Briscoe]] (brother)
[[Jack Briscoe]] (brother) }} }} '''Luke Briscoe''' (born 11 March 1994) is an English [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|wg}}er or {{rlp|ce}} for [[Doncaster RLFC|Doncaster]] in the [[RFL Championship]]. He started his career at the [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] club, playing on loan at [[Wakefield Trinity]] in the [[Super League]], [[Hunslet R.L.F.C.|Hunslet]] in [[RFL League 1|Championship 1]] and the [[Kingstone Press Championship]], and [[Featherstone Rovers|Featherstone]] in the [[RFL Championship|Kingstone Press Championship]]. He played for [[Featherstone Rovers]] in the Championship and joined [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] on loan in the top flight. He then spent time away from [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] on [[Rugby League Dual registration|dual registration]] with [[Featherstone Rovers]] in the [[RFL Championship|Betfred Championship]]. ==Background== Briscoe was born in [[Featherstone]], [[West Yorkshire]], England. He is the brother of [[England national rugby league team]] winger [[Tom Briscoe]] who also played for the [[Leeds Rhinos]].{{citation needed|date=January 2023}} ==Career== ===Leeds=== He is signed for the 2012 season onwards, he signed a professional contract 22 September 2011. He joined [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] on 22 September 2012, after playing his youth career at [[Hull F.C.]] He signed a four-year contract with the club. He made three appearances for Leeds and scored once on his début. He was released at the end of the 2015 season.{{citation needed|date=January 2023}} ===Wakefield Trinity=== Briscoe spent the 2014 Super League season at [[Wakefield Trinity]] on a one-year loan deal. However, he only made two appearances and returned to Leeds.{{citation needed|date=February 2018}} ===Hunslet RLFC=== Briscoe was sent on loan to Hunslet for the rest of the season and played 18 games, scoring 6 tries.{{citation needed|date=February 2018}} ===Leeds Rhinos=== On 19 June 2018 it was reported that he had signed for [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] in the [[Super League]].{{cite web|website=Yorkshire Evening Post|date=19 Jun 2018|accessdate=23 Jan 2021|title=Leeds Rhinos sign record try scorer Luke Briscoe from Featherstone Rovers|url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/leeds-rhinos-sign-record-try-scorer-luke-briscoe-featherstone-rovers-584450}} ===Featherstone Rovers (re-join)=== On 27 October 2021 it was reported that he had signed for [[Featherstone Rovers]] in the [[RFL Championship]] in a permanent move{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=27 Oct 2021|accessdate=26 Dec 2021|title=Luke Briscoe "honoured" to return to hometown club|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/luke-briscoe-honoured-to-return-to-hometown-club-featherstone/}} On 28 May 2022, Briscoe played for Featherstone in their [[2022 RFL 1895 Cup]] final loss against [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]].{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/featherstone-rovers-1895-cup-final-defeat-to-leigh-centurions-gives-brian-mcdermotts-side-signal-of-their-progress-3713313/|title=Featherstone Rovers: 1895 Cup final defeat to Leigh Centurions gives Brian McDermott's side signal of their progress|website=www.yorkshirepost.co.uk}} ===Doncaster RLFC=== On 10 November 2023 it was reported that he had signed for [[Doncaster RLFC]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on a two-year deal.{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=10 Nov 2023|title=Former Leeds Rhinos ace makes cross-county Championship switch following departure from Featherstone Rovers|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/former-leeds-rhinos-ace-makes-cross-county-championship-switch-following-departure-from-featherstone-rovers}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External
